steps.title: Identify the appositive
steps.content: The compound names and formulas “hexafluorobenzene (C 6 F 6) and undecane (C 11 H 24)” form an appositive phrase—a group of words that identifies which compounds we're discussing. This identification is essential; without these specific names, “the liquid compounds” would be incomplete.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content: Appositives that identify a noun (restrictive) take no commas; appositives that add extra info about an already-identified noun (non-restrictive) take commas. Since these compound names are essential for specifying which compounds have those molar masses, the appositive is restrictive. Choices A, B, and D all incorrectly include commas—either as a pair (B) or mismatched (A, D)—treating it as non-restrictive. That matches choice C — C is correct.
